scope: AgenC app-server websocket transport that accepts remote daemon clients over a TCP websocket endpoint while preserving the donor server accept loop behaviors that fit the local daemon architecture.
_sourceRootBrandingAllow: branding-scan: allow donor source root path

requiredBehaviors: bind a loopback websocket server and expose the actual listen URL when using an ephemeral port
accept websocket text frames as JSON object daemon payloads
send JSON responses over the same websocket connection without newline framing
keep each websocket connection isolated behind a stable numeric connection id
edgeCases: malformed JSON reports an error without tearing down the connection
sending on a closed connection rejects

requiredBehaviors: serve health and readiness probes over HTTP on the websocket listener
reject websocket upgrades that include a browser Origin header by default
allow callers to provide an explicit origin validator for trusted browser clients
edgeCases: readiness can return a non-ready status
wrong websocket paths reject during HTTP upgrade

requiredBehaviors: close active websocket clients when the server shuts down
run the connection-closed callback once per accepted websocket
wait for pending message handlers before close returns
edgeCases: close is idempotent before or after listen

requiredBehaviors: do not create a hosted remote-control placeholder without the backing control service
keep the websocket server API extensible through caller-supplied origin policy and daemon dispatcher wiring
record the donor remote-control files as read source anchors for future portal and IDE connection work
edgeCases: hosted enrollment URLs are not hard-coded
backend-specific bearer-token recovery is not guessed
remote virtual-client stream replay is deferred until the daemon has a real hosted control endpoint
